pii_fields.description: User's email address used as login username (e.g. demouser@microsoft.com). Directly identifies an individual. Also propagated to CatalogDB as Baskets.BuyerId and Orders.BuyerId.
User's email address (same value as UserName in this application).
ASP.NET Identity PBKDF2 password hash. Not plaintext.
pii_fields.encryption: Hashed — PBKDF2 via ASP.NET Identity. Not reversible.

pii_fields.description: Phone number if collected. Not required — nullable. Not exposed in demo UI.

For authenticated users: email address (e.g. demouser@microsoft.com). For anonymous users: GUID string (not inherently PII but stored for up to 10 years via cookie). Email value directly identifies the person.
Email address of customer who placed the order. Permanent historical record.
Full shipping address. High PII sensitivity — physical location of customer. In demo app, all orders use a hardcoded test address. In production this would contain real customer addresses.
Order line items linked to an Order (which has BuyerId). Constitutes behavioral/transactional data about a specific individual. Permanent historical record.

⚠️ SHADOW ENTITY — Buyer and PaymentMethod are defined in domain code (src/ApplicationCore/Entities/BuyerAggregate/) but are NOT mapped in CatalogContext and NOT included in any migration. These tables do NOT exist in the database. Code comment: 'actual card data must be stored in a PCI compliant system, like Stripe'. Confirmed dead code — never instantiated in any service, repository, or test file.
pii_fields.confidence_note: 0.85 because live DB not connected to confirm no shadow tables were created manually outside migrations.

agent2_review_changes.what: Cookie IsEssential=true IS set (GDPR lawful basis), but Secure and HttpOnly are NOT. Test suite (CheckoutTest.cs) confirms hardcoded demo credentials 'demouser@microsoft.com' / 'Pass@word1' are used in functional tests, tightly coupling tests to the constant — cannot rotate password without updating tests.
agent2_review_changes.evidence_detail: src/Web/Pages/Basket/Index.cshtml.cs:94 — IsEssential=true confirmed. tests/FunctionalTests/Web/Pages/Basket/CheckoutTest.cs:48 — hardcoded 'Pass@word1' and 'demouser@microsoft.com'. tests/PublicApiIntegrationTests/ApiTokenHelper.cs:14 — hardcoded 'admin@microsoft.com' and AuthorizationConstants.JWT_SECRET_KEY used to mint test tokens.

pii_risk_notes: No column-level encryption-at-rest observed for PII fields in CatalogDB or IdentityDB beyond ASP.NET Identity's password hashing.
BuyerId (email) is denormalized into CatalogDB (Baskets, Orders) with no FK to IdentityDB — a right-to-erasure request requires manual cleanup across both databases.
Anonymous basket GUIDs persist in Baskets table indefinitely — no TTL or cleanup job observed in codebase.
JWT_SECRET_KEY is hardcoded in AuthorizationConstants.cs (TODO: 'Change this to an environment variable'). Exposure of this key allows forging admin JWT tokens.
DEFAULT_PASSWORD is hardcoded in AuthorizationConstants.cs (TODO: 'Don't use this in production'). Both seeded accounts share this password.
All test suites use in-memory databases — no test validates data isolation or cleanup policies against SQL Server.
